assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All 4 slices will be recreated entirely in a single step' and 'this is not a rolling upgrade', and contrasts this with standard node upgrades in the comparison table

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'The maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices simultaneously' under the Cascading restart section

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Complete job termination: Your LLM training run will be terminated' and 'Pod eviction from all TPU slices simultaneously' with 'All training pods terminated' in the comparison table

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response addresses this with 'If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist during recreation, slices block until hardware is available — this can extend downtime significantly' and mentions 'Slice recreation may be delayed hours/days until v5e hardware is available'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides specific command 'kubectl exec -it TRAINING_POD -- python save_checkpoint.py --force-save' and recommends 'Force frequent checkpoints before maintenance' with code examples

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes code examples for checkpoint configuration with 'checkpoint_manager.force_save_on_preemption = True' and provides restart validation procedures with checkpoint restore verification

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer maintenance events. It focuses on preparation and recovery but not on postponement options

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response includes a 'Maintenance Window Planning' section that recommends scheduling 'during training gaps' and 'Natural checkpoint boundaries (end of epoch)' and 'Weekend/off-peak hours'
